title: Odontochilus crispus
intro: Odontochilus crispus, also known as the Chinese toothed orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Odontochilus crispus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, such as iridoid glycosides and phenylethanoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is used as a treatment for various ailments, including coughs, fever, and sore throats.
